title: astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus
intro: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus, commonly known as Purple Astragalus, is a plant species within the legume family, native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The therapeutic actions of Purple Astragalus are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Research on Purple Astragalus suggests that its bioactive constituents may help to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions.
benefits: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus has many benefits, such as its potential to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and triglycerides, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, this plant has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against various bacterial and fungal infections, and it may also help to regulate blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes. The antioxidants present in Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases. By incorporating this plant into one's diet or using it as a supplement, individuals may experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to a better quality of life and increased vitality.
actions: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with conditions like arthritis, allowing individuals to engage in daily activities like hiking and gardening without discomfort. Its immunomodulatory effects also enable the plant to boost the immune system, protecting people from illnesses and infections, thus enabling them to maintain a healthy work-life balance and participate in social events without worrying about getting sick. Additionally, the plant's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, enabling individuals to manage their mental well-being and perform tasks with greater focus and productivity, such as working on projects and managing a household. By incorporating Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus into their daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall health and well-being, allowing them to lead more fulfilling and active lifestyles.
constituents: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have been found to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and improving lipid profiles. The phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have been demonstrated to exhibit antioxidant activity, protecting against cell damage and improving cognitive function by scavenging free radicals and improving neuroprotection. The immunomodulatory effects of the alkaloids, such as astragaline and ononin, have been found to enhance immune function, reducing the severity of respiratory infections and improving overall health and well-being.
parts: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The roots of Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant contain phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid and chicoric acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
preparations: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus has many herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are used for various medicinal purposes. For instance, teas made from the plant are used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, while tinctures are used to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Infusions and decoctions are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, by loosening mucus and reducing congestion. Additionally, astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus root is sometimes made into a powder that can be used as a dietary supplement to boost energy and vitality.
side_effects: Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing in individuals with sensitivities. Excessive consumption may lead to gastrointestinal issues like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as the plant contains compounds that can be toxic in large quantities. Additionally, using Astragalus onobrychis subsp. purpureus for extended periods can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially causing adverse effects. In rare cases, high doses may also cause kidney or liver damage, highlighting the importance of proper dosage and medical supervision.
